rulings.Case Title: IN RE: RALPH F. GESCAT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Relief From Government Entity Claim Requirements [Gov. Code 945.4, 946.6]; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2024, Line 1. PETITIONER RALPH GESCAT, AS THE SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST AND PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF RICHARD BOYD STAPP GESCAT, DECEASED Motion For Relief From Government Entity Claim Requirements [Gov. Code 945.4, 946.6]. Petitioner's motion for relief from the government claims requirement is granted. Petitioner demonstrates excusable neglect under Government Code section 946.6 and respondents fail to demonstrate prejudice to warrant the denial of relief. "Section 946.6 is a remedial statute intended to provide relief from technical rules that otherwise provide a trap for the unwary claimant. As such, it is construed in favor of relief whenever possible." (Bettencourt v. Los Rios Community College Dist. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 270, 275.) Here, petitioner attempted to procure counsel during the claims period and was rebuffed. He also experienced severe emotional distress and was waiting to figure out the cause of the fire. (Gescat Decl., pars. 4, 9-12; Alvarez Decl., 7-8.) Petitioner demonstrates good cause for relief and respondents fail to demonstrate prejudice. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

Defendants' demurrers to the complaint under Code of Civil Procedure 430.10(e&f) are sustained with leave to amend. rulings.Case Title: JOAO NUGENT RAMOS PINTO ET AL VS. MARY FONG LAU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; And Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2024, Line 14. DEFENDANT MARY LAU's Motion To Stay. Defendant's motion to stay this litigation as to her is granted. Defendant killed plaintiffs' family members in a March 2024 collision at a bus stop; she faces felony charges in a criminal case and tort allegations in this case. The impact on defendant's self-incrimination rights from civil discovery and trial is obvious. (Evid. Code 940; Pacers, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 162 Cal.App.3d 686, 688-89.) Plaintiffs say they "have a compelling interest in prosecuting this action to justice before other witnesses' memories fade further and documents are gone." (Opp. 2:2-4.) But the possibility of documents disappearing in our electronic age is remote, and plaintiffs may move to lift all or part of the stay if the criminal case stalls. Barring that, the stay shall remain until the criminal case ends. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE CHRISTY VS. GOLDEN GATEIDENCE OPCO, LLC D/B/A VICTORIAN POST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2024, Line 11. DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATEIDENCE OPCO, LLC D/B/A VICTORIAN POST ACUTE, BAY AREA MASTER TENANT LLC, PROVIDENCE GROUP, INC., PACS GROUP INC., AND PACS HOLDINGS, LLC's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Defendants' unopposed motion to compel arbitration and stay civil action is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
